methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $256K total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Terran Robotics is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Zach Dwiel and Danny Weddle that has raised $256K to date. The robotics and manufacturing sector includes 9 private peers and 1 public comparable, though no same-stage or adjacent-stage peers within the narrow robotics subsegment possess sufficient data density for traditional peer-median valuation.

The stage-progression method applies here as the primary triangulation approach. Using total capital raised ($256K) as a value proxy and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $640K. This method is grounded in observed step-up mechanics across early-stage funding rounds in the sector. Peer-median analysis was inapplicable due to insufficient comparable data points at equivalent stages; the broader sector contains 11 robotics peers but none clustered tightly enough on stage and valuation tier to support a median calculation without material distortion.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $544K (low) to $736K (high), centered on $640K. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single applicable method rather than triangulated convergence across multiple approaches. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 2.5x stage multiplier for Terran's specific subsector and capital efficiency, the absence of product-stage revenue or traction metrics, and limited peer data at the Seed stage. Additional customer validation or near-term financing terms would materially refine this estimate.
